question: A ball of mass 0.20 kg is thrown vertically downwards at an initial speed of 4.0 m s$^{-1}$ and travels a distance of 0.45 m to the ground. The ball hits the ground, and rebounds with an initial speed of 2.0 m s$^{-1}$. How much energy does the ball lose in the bounce? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; air resistance can be ignored)
_note: Flash had put printed q52 (load/pulley, PDF page 42) into q42. Real q42 is ball-bounce on PDF page 35.
